data.title: RBB Bancorp announces Johnny Lee as the new CEO
data.description:
• RBB Bancorp (NASDAQ:RBB) announced that effective January 1, 2025, Johnny Lee, currently president and chief banking officer of Royal Business Bank, will be appointed as president of the company, president and chief executive officer of the bank and a director of the company and bank.
  • Effective as of May 22, 2025, Lee will be appointed as chief executive officer of the company.
  • Effective January 1, 2025, David Morris, will resign from his position as chief executive officer of the bank and president of the company.
  • Morris will continue serving as the company’s chief executive officer until he retires, effective as of May 21, 2025.
  • Morris will remain on the boards of directors of the company and the bank.
  • Lee, most recently, served as senior managing director, head of international and commercial banking of East West Bank, from 2021 to 2023.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: enGene Holdings GAAP EPS of -$1.46 misses by $0.03
data.description:
• enGene Holdings press release (NASDAQ:ENGN): FY GAAP EPS of -$1.46 misses by $0.03.
  • BLA filing for detalimogene in BCG-unresponsive NMIBC with CIS on track for mid-2026
  • All cohorts of the Phase 2 LEGEND study now recruiting under updated protocol
  • Additional preliminary data from pivotal cohort anticipated in 2H 2025
  • Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities of $297.9 million provide runway into 2027.

data.title: Stifel's November client assets buoyed by strong stock markets, financial advisor recruiting
data.description:
Stifel Financial's (NYSE:SF) total client assets rose 4% sequentially and 20% from a year ago to $513.9B at Nov. 30, 2024, bolstered by strong equity markets and financial advisor recruiting, the company said on Thursday.

Bank loans, net (includes loans held for sale) were $20.7B, virtually unchanged from the previous month and up from $20.3B in the same period a year earlier.

Client money market and insured products of $28.6B compared with $27.6B in at Oct. 31, 2024, and $25.5B at Nov. 30, 2023.

data.title: HF Foods Group files to sell 5.2 million shares of common stock by selling shareholders
data.description:
• HF Foods Group (NASDAQ:HFFG) filed a prospectus related to the offer and resale of up to about 5.2 million shares of common stock.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: Mister Car Wash transfers stock listing to Nasdaq from NYSE
data.description:
• Mister Car Wash (NYSE:MCW) announced that it will voluntarily transfer the listing of its common stock from the New York Stock Exchange to Nasdaq effective December 31, 2024, after market close.
  • The company expects its common stock to begin trading on Nasdaq upon market open on January 2, 2025, and continue to trade under the ticker symbol "MCW".
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: FedEx Freight to be separated and listed as a new public company
data.description:
Parcel delivery giant FedEx (NYSE:FDX) said Thursday it has decided to pursue a tax-free separation of FedEx Freight, creating two publicly-listed companies.

Shares of the company rose more than 10% in after-hours trading on the NYSE. Shares of rival UPS (UPS) were up 1%.

“This is the right time to pursue a separation as we respond to the unique dynamics of the LTL market...Through this process, we will unlock value for our Freight business and position FedEx to create even greater value for stockholders, CEO Raj Subramaniam said in a press release.

The separation is expected to be carried out in the next 18 months, the company said.

FedEx Freight reported $9.4B in revenue this year and is expected to benefit from "a strong balance sheet," according to the company. No executives have been named who would be part of the new company.

Goldman Sachs is serving as the company's financial advisor for the separation.

data.title: Seacoast Banking Corporation of Florida announces renewed $100M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Seacoast Banking Corporation of Florida (NASDAQ:SBCF) board renewed the company's share repurchase program, which was set to expire on December 31, 2024.
  • Under the renewed repurchase program, which will expire on December 31, 2025, the company may repurchase, up to $100M of its shares of common stock.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bowman secures contracts for geospatial data collection in Texas
data.description:
• Bowman Consulting Group (NASDAQ:BWMN) said on Thursday that it has secured two significant contracts, with a combined value of nearly $900,000, to provide asset management and data collection services for an exploration and production company in the Permian Basin region of Texas.
  • The projects include work across 973 total locations, consisting of both facilities and well sites in the Midland Basin’s North and South regions, situated within the broader Permian Basin.
  • The company will also provide project management oversight and regular progress reporting to further support the client’s asset management initiatives.
  • Field mobilization is currently underway, with project completion expected by July 2025.

data.title: FCPT acquires a P.F. Chang's property for $4.8M
data.description:
• Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT), on Thursday, announced the acquisition of a P.F. Chang's property for $4.8 million.
  • P.F. Chang’s China Bistro is an American-based, casual dining restaurant chain with over 200 locations across the United States, serving Asian fusion cuisine.
  • The property is located in a highly trafficked, strong retail corridor in Illinois and under a corporate triple net lease with approximately 5 years of term remaining.
  • The transaction was priced at a 7.4% cap rate on rent as of the closing date and exclusive of transaction costs.

data.title: Wall Street rebound fizzles out, but Dow snaps longest losing streak since 1974
data.description:
Wall Street's bounce back on Thursday following its worst session in over three months ran out of steam in the final hour of trading. Market participants received data that showed strong U.S. economic growth, after having a night to mull over the Federal Reserve's hawkish interest rate cut.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) gained 1% immediately after the open, but then steadily pared that advance through the day. It eventually ended -0.09% at 5,867.08 points. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) followed a similar pattern, finally settling -0.10% at 19,372.77 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) eked out a marginal rise of +0.04% to conclude at 42,342.24 points.

Of the 11 S&P sectors, seven closed in the red, led by Real Estate. Utilities topped the winners.

Over in the fixed-income markets, Treasury yields were mixed. Longer-end maturities climbed, with the 10-year yield (US10Y) up 5 basis points to 4.57%. Shorter-end instruments slipped, with the 2-year yield (US2Y) down 4 basis points to 4.32%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

The Fed on Wednesday delivered a 25 basis point rate cut, as widely expected. But it was the central bank's updated Summary of Economic Projections, or dot plot, that was the story of the day. The Fed now sees higher inflation and fewer rate cuts in 2025, with chair Jerome Powell stressing caution going forward.

Markets reacted in an ugly manner, with the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) eventually sinking more than 3%.

The mood was much calmer on Thursday, with a busy economic calendar grabbing attention.

“Today’s market action was a bit of a relief after yesterday’s carnage. Nonetheless, we’re in a new market environment, as the Fed has made clear that inflation is indeed stickier than expected. As a result, some expect rate hikes next year if the CPI rate increases of last month turn out to be the start of a bottoming process," Leo Nelissen, part of investing group iREIT+HOYA Capital, told Seeking Alpha.

"This can become a problem if higher rates hurt growth expectations, as the market trades north of 22x forward earnings based on 2025 S&P 500 EPS growth expectations of 15%. This has made the risk/reward unfavorable and could keep returns subdued in 2025," Nelissen added.

Before the opening bell, the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis said real gross domestic product (GDP) improved at an annual rate of 3.1% in Q3 2024, a higher reading than the previous estimate of +2.8%. The update indicated that the U.S. economy remained robust, but was also another reminder that perhaps more easing from the Fed is not immediately required.

At the same time as the GDP estimate, data from the U.S. Department of Labor showed that the number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims in the past week fell more than expected.

Also, in a notable development, the Conference Board's U.S. leading indicator index rose in November for the first time since February 2022. The index has long been seen as a recession bellwether, however its divergence over the last two years or so from a largely healthy economy had called that status into question.

Turning to Thursday's active stocks, Micron Technology (MU) slumped -16.2% and ended as the top percentage loser on the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND). The chipmaker's current quarter guidance disappointed investors, and so did its quarterly revenue decline in NAND flash memory.

The world's largest shoe company, Nike (NKE), and global economic bellwether FedEx (FDX) will be reporting results after the closing bell.
